plot_hooks.title: A Favor for a Little Charm
plot_hooks.setup: Mother Hallow offers the PC a charm that actually works—a warded token that grants a small but tangible benefit (bonus vs. fear, protecting a mortal, or masking aura). The charm is 'free'… for now.
plot_hooks.hook: A night or two later, she calls in a favor: retrieve a specific item, deliver a warning, or escort a jittery mortal client who owes her. The task seems mundane on the surface, but all parties involved are a little too aware of the supernatural.
objectives: Accept or refuse the charm (and understand it really does something)
Complete the errand without revealing Mother Hallow’s deeper involvement
Decide whether to keep, return, or abuse the charm’s power
complications: The person or item Mother Hallow wants is already under surveillance by another faction
Using the charm leaves a subtle 'signature' certain entities can sense
Refusing future favors may sour Mother Hallow’s attitude toward the PC
potential_outcomes: PC secures a working occult charm and gains Mother Hallow’s cautious favor
Another faction notices the charm’s effect and starts asking questions
Mother Hallow quietly marks the PC as 'in her ledger' for future, bigger bargains

plot_hooks.title: Written in the Wrong Name
plot_hooks.setup: The PC (or an ally) discovers that Mother Hallow keeps a private ledger of 'customers' behind the counter. The entries don’t match the names people gave her—they match something closer to True Names.
plot_hooks.hook: An NPC whose entry appears in the ledger starts suffering strange bad luck, nightmares, or compulsion. They beg the PC for help, or the PC becomes the next name written in the book.
objectives: Confirm that the ledger is more than mundane bookkeeping
Determine what power the True Name inscription grants Mother Hallow (or something she serves)
Confront her, steal the ledger, or negotiate terms for altering or erasing a name
complications: Tampering with the ledger triggers supernatural backlash (spirits, curses, or attention from her patron)
Mother Hallow warns that erasing a name may erase more than misfortune
Other NPCs whose names are in the ledger become collateral in any conflict
potential_outcomes: PC removes or alters an entry, gaining a powerful ally—or losing a piece of themselves
Mother Hallow tightens her hold, insisting that written names cannot simply be undone
The ledger’s magic is revealed as part of a larger network of bindings in Phoenix

plot_hooks.title: The Spirits Between the Shelves
plot_hooks.setup: With Auspex or heightened sensitivity, the PC perceives that dust motes and shadows in certain aisles of Mother Hallow’s shop orbit unseen anchors. Bound spirits linger in the stacks, restless but unable to leave.
plot_hooks.hook: One of these spirits tries to communicate—a dead occultist, a former client, maybe even a Kindred—or it simply lashes out. The PC must decide whether to help, exploit, or ignore these trapped entities.
objectives: Identify at least one bound spirit and learn why it remains in the shop
Discover the anchor: an item, a sigil, a contract, or Mother Hallow’s will
Choose whether to free the spirit, rebind it to a new anchor, or weaponize it
complications: Freeing a spirit might unleash dangerous knowledge or vengeance
Mother Hallow notices disturbances in her 'quiet guests' and is not amused
Other spirits beg for release once the PC proves they can interfere
potential_outcomes: PC gains a grateful ghostly contact—or a vengeful enemy released into the world
Mother Hallow treats the PC as either a loose cannon or a potential assistant in spirit-work
The bound spirits hint at deeper secrets in Phoenix, connecting to other plots (Giovanni, mages, or the dead Prince)

plot_hooks.title: The Memory That’s Missing
plot_hooks.setup: A mortal or minor ghoul comes to the PC in distress. They visited Mother Hallow’s shop, paid for something… and now there’s a blank space in their memory. The missing time is small—but uncomfortably precise.
plot_hooks.hook: Tracing the gap leads back to Mother Hallow, who calmly explains that sometimes money isn’t enough. She doesn’t deny taking memories—and she might offer the same 'payment method' to the PC.
objectives: Investigate the missing time and confirm Mother Hallow’s role
Decide whether to pressure her, bargain with her, or accept her terms
Optionally attempt to restore the memory or trade something else instead
complications: Restoring the memory may reveal something the victim didn’t want to know
Mother Hallow hints that the memory was taken to protect them—or the PC
If the PC accepts a memory trade, the gap might be strategically placed later in the chronicle
potential_outcomes: The PC recovers the memory at a price, learning a dangerous secret
The PC chooses to sacrifice one of their own memories for power, safety, or information
Mother Hallow begins treating the PC as a serious client in her more dangerous bargains

plot_hooks.title: She Knew Before Elysium
plot_hooks.setup: Rumors circulate that Mother Hallow knew the Prince of Phoenix was dead before anyone brought word to Elysium. Some say she closed her shop early that night. Others claim she lit extra candles and waited.
plot_hooks.hook: A Camarilla functionary, Harpy’s Talon, or Tremere investigator pressures the PC to verify whether Mother Hallow had foreknowledge, and if so, how. Alternatively, she herself hints to the PC that the Prince’s death was only the first omen.
objectives: Visit Mother Hallow and subtly probe what she knew and when
Determine whether her 'omen' came from divination, spirits, or a more mundane leak
Choose whether to report her to Camarilla authorities, shield her, or leverage the information
complications: If the Tremere sniff Thaumaturgy, they may stake a claim on her or her sources
Mother Hallow might deliberately give conflicting accounts to test the PC’s discretion
The real source of the omen may implicate another powerful NPC or faction
potential_outcomes: PC secures Mother Hallow as a discreet prophetic ally
Camarilla scrutiny tightens around her shop, making it riskier to visit
The omen reveals hints of a larger pattern of deaths or disasters in the Valley

plot_hooks.title: The Door That Never Opens
plot_hooks.setup: Word spreads that the back room of Mother Hallow’s shop is never locked, but no one has ever actually seen inside. Every attempt to casually push the door open is met with a sudden jolt of animal terror.
plot_hooks.hook: The PC needs something only stored 'in the back'—or is hired by another NPC to find out what’s behind that door. Mother Hallow might offer access as part of a dangerous bargain—or the PC may try to sneak in.
objectives: Overcome the supernatural fear or compulsion that keeps most people from the door
Enter the hidden room without triggering catastrophic wards
Discover what Mother Hallow keeps there: rare artifacts, bound entities, or her true workshop
complications: The room itself may be larger on the inside or partially in another realm
Crossing the threshold might set off alarms heard by spirits, not mortals
Something inside may notice the PC more than they notice it
potential_outcomes: PC gains access to unique occult resources—or a new permanent risk
Mother Hallow reacts based on whether they were invited, bargained, or broke in
The hidden room reveals tangible evidence of her patron or greater designs

plot_hooks.title: Shelves After Midnight
plot_hooks.setup: It becomes clear that Mother Hallow has 'night shelves'—items she only shows to Kindred and other true night-folk. Stakes threaded with runes, phials of something that isn’t quite blood, talismans keyed to Beast and Hunger.
plot_hooks.hook: Mother Hallow offers the PC access to this private stock… if they prove themselves. That proof might be a small errand, a secret traded, or a demonstration of control over their Beast.
objectives: Demonstrate to Mother Hallow that the PC is worth trusting with restricted items
Select one or more items, each with clear benefits and subtle strings attached
Deal with the consequences of using such an item in the field
complications: Other Kindred notice the PC using something that 'smells' like Mother Hallow’s work
Some items may resonate with certain clans or Disciplines more than others
Mother Hallow tracks who buys what—she expects stories in return
potential_outcomes: PC gains unique gear that can tilt social or supernatural encounters
Mother Hallow becomes a key supplier—and lever—in the PC’s unlife
A rival NPC becomes jealous of the PC’s access and starts maneuvering against them

plot_hooks.title: Ink That Remembers
plot_hooks.setup: The PC or an ally notices sigils forming in the ink of a receipt from Mother Hallow’s shop—symbols that weren’t visible before. Shortly afterward, something begins tracking or targeting whoever holds that slip of paper.
plot_hooks.hook: Attacks, bad luck, or eerie coincidences cluster around the receipt’s owner. Mother Hallow hints (or denies) that her receipts can be used as sympathetic links—for protection, for tracking, or for harm.
objectives: Identify the connection between the receipt and the escalating events
Discover who—or what—is using the sympathetic link and to what end
Destroy, cleanse, invert, or repurpose the link for the PC’s advantage
complications: Destroying the receipt may not be enough; the ink or sigil pattern might now cling to the aura
Another occultist or rival has stolen a stack of old receipts for their own use
Mother Hallow insists this wasn’t her doing… entirely
potential_outcomes: PC breaks the link and prevents further harm, gaining insight into occult warfare in Phoenix
PC turns the link back on its user, setting up a future confrontation
Mother Hallow quietly notes how the PC handled it and offers 'more advanced work'

plot_hooks.title: The Chain Above Her
plot_hooks.setup: Over multiple visits and smaller bargains, a picture emerges: Mother Hallow does not ultimately answer to Camarilla, Anarchs, Sabbat, or any known clan. Her language occasionally slips—'my employer,' 'the one upstream,' 'the hand behind the shelf.'
plot_hooks.hook: Patterns in her omens, bound spirits, and ledgers point toward a larger power: a powerful spirit, an elder sorcerer, a distant cult, or something stranger. The PC has a chance to tug on that chain and see who—or what—tugs back.
objectives: Collect and interpret clues from previous Mother Hallow encounters (omens, contracts, names, spirits)
Force, negotiate, or earn a glimpse of the patron behind her work
Decide whether to leave the chain alone, cut it, or climb it
complications: The patron may notice the PC long before the PC understands what it is
Interfering with the arrangement could destabilize Mother Hallow—and her protections on the city
Other factions may want the PC to act as their proxy in dealing with her patron
potential_outcomes: PC earns a terrifying but potent favor from something above Mother Hallow
Mother Hallow’s chain is damaged, making her more independent—but also more vulnerable and volatile
The patron sets a condition or warning that echoes into the final arc of the chronicle

meta_hooks.title: The Shop as Occult Hub
meta_hooks.description: Use Mother Hallow’s shop as a recurring hub for occult errands, upgrades, and rumors. Early on, keep stakes small and transactional. As the PC’s ties deepen, layer in hints of bound spirits, true names, and her unseen patron.

meta_hooks.title: Escalating Costs
meta_hooks.description: Start with simple purchases and minor favors, then move to payments in memories, names, or spiritual interference. Track what the PC has given up; let those bargains echo back in later scenes and plots.

meta_hooks.title: Her Ledger vs. Their Ledger
meta_hooks.description: Mother Hallow’s ledgers, receipts, and bindings can cross streams with Giovanni necromancy, Tremere wards, and Setite cult networks. Use her as a bridge NPC who can comment on or meddle with other factions’ occult infrastructure.

usage_notes.scaling: Introduce Mother Hallow’s plots as low-risk favors and unsettling incidents in the early nights. Transition into heavier memory, spirit, and patron plots once the PC trusts—or fears—her enough to keep going back.

difficulty_guide.Easy: Small errands, single-scene bargains, minor but flavorful rewards.
difficulty_guide.Medium: Multi-step investigations, social pressure from factions, spirits or curses with manageable fallout.
difficulty_guide.Hard: Long-term occult entanglements, patron-level attention, choices that permanently alter PC resources or identity.

usage_notes.combination: Use 'A Favor for a Little Charm' as the PC’s entry point, then chain into 'The Memory That’s Missing' or 'Ink That Remembers' to show the darker side of her craft. 'The Door That Never Opens' and 'The Chain Above Her' serve as mid-to-late chronicle reveals for players who keep pulling on the thread.
usage_notes.character_growth: Success in Mother Hallow’s plots should teach the PC that power always has a price—and that information, memories, and spirits are currencies. Failure, or reckless meddling, should leave marks: gaps in memory, new spiritual ties, or a reputation as someone who meddles with forces older than Phoenix itself.
